children.heading: Secret ballot election; majority vote
children.label: a Secret ballot election; majority vote
children.text: The Department shall accord exclusive recognition to a labor organization if the organization has been selected as the representative, in a secret ballot election, by a majority of the employees in a unit who cast valid ballots in the election.

children.heading: Investigation of petition; hearing; supervision of election; certification of results; length of time between elections
children.label: b Investigation of petition; hearing; supervision of election; certification of results; length of time between elections
A
children.text: in the case of a unit for which there is no exclusive representative, that 30 percent of the employees in the unit wish to be represented for the purpose of collective bargaining by an exclusive representative, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a unit for which there is an exclusive representative, that 30 percent of the employees in the unit alleged that the exclusive representative is no longer the representative of the majority of the employees in the unit; or
by any person seeking clarification of, or an amendment to, a certification then in effect or a matter relating to representation;

children.heading: Intervention of labor organizations; placement on ballot
children.label: c Intervention of labor organizations; placement on ballot

children.text: has been designated by at least 10 percent of the employees in the unit; or
is the exclusive representative of the employees involved;

children.heading: Eligibility to vote; regulations; choices on ballot; preferential voting; certification as exclusive representative
children.label: d Eligibility to vote; regulations; choices on ballot; preferential voting; certification as exclusive representative
A
children.text: from labor organizations on the ballot, that labor organization which the employees wish to have represent them; or

children.label: B
children.text: not to be represented by a labor organization.

In any election in which more than two choices are on the ballot, the regulations of the Board shall provide for preferential voting. If no choice receives a majority of first preferences, the Board shall distribute to the two choices having the most first preferences the preferences as between those two of the other valid ballots cast. The choice receiving a majority of preferences shall be declared the winner. A labor organization which is declared the winner of the election shall be certified by the Board as the exclusive representative.

children.heading: Submission of required material
children.label: e Submission of required material
children.text: A labor organization seeking exclusive recognition shall submit to the Board and to the Department a roster of its officers and representatives, a copy of its constitution and bylaws, and a statement of its objectives.

children.heading: Grounds for denial of exclusive recognition status
children.label: f Grounds for denial of exclusive recognition status

children.text: if the Board determines that the labor organization is subject to corrupt influence or influences opposed to democratic principles; or

in the case of a petition filed under subsection (b)(1)(A), if there is not credible evidence that at least 30 percent of the employees wish to be represented for the purpose of collective bargaining by the labor organization seeking exclusive recognition.

children.heading: Waiver of hearings; consent elections
children.label: g Waiver of hearings; consent elections
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to prohibit the waiving of hearings by stipulation for the purpose of a consent election in conformity with regulations and rules or decisions of the Board.
